page.content: Veolia Environnement's Fiscal Year 2024 report outlines critical data on recycling recovery rates and circular economy material flows.

lines.text: Veolia Environnement's Fiscal Year 2024 report outlines critical data on recycling recovery rates and circular economy material

page.content: The first table reveals that the material types include Plastics with a recovery rate of 42.5% and revenue of $25M, Metals at 65.0% and $30M, Paper at 55.0% and $20M, Glass at 50.0% and $15M, OrganicWaste at 35.0% and $10M, E-Waste at 60.0% and $5M, culminating in a TotalRecovery rate of 51.5% with total revenue of $105M. The second table details RecycledMaterials, reporting an amount of $200M with a Year-over-Year Change of 25%. The RecyclingRate reflects a WasteGenerated figure of $150M, with a LandfillDiversion rate of 30%. For individual materials, Plastics amount to $80M with a performance metric of 15% and a market share of 25%, Metals at $50M with a performance metric of 10% and a market share of 20%, OrganicWaste at $70M with a performance metric of 20% and a market share of 15%, and Electronics at $30M with a performance metric of 5% and a market share of 10%.

page.content: This comprehensive overview highlights the financial and operational metrics essential for assessing the effectiveness of recycling initiatives and materials recovery in the circular economy.
